quest_intro.speaker: Scout_Davan
quest_intro.text: The big ones are moving. I have been tracking them for three days — they follow the river south, same as last dry season.

quest_intro.speaker: Scout_Davan
quest_intro.text: If we know where the herd crosses, we know where the predators wait. And where the predators wait, we do not camp.

quest_intro.speaker: Scout_Davan
quest_intro.text: Follow them to the four waypoints I marked. Do not get close — a spooked herd is more dangerous than any predator. Just watch.

objective_progress.speaker: Scout_Davan
objective_progress.text: Good — you found the grazing ground. See how they spread out? That means no predators within half a day's walk. Safe ground.

objective_progress.speaker: Scout_Davan
objective_progress.text: The river crossing. Watch the lead animal — the one with the scarred neck. Where it steps, the others follow. That is the safe ford.

objective_progress.speaker: Scout_Davan
objective_progress.text: They are bunching together. Something spooked them from the east. Stay low — do not let them see you as a threat.

objective_progress.speaker: Scout_Davan
objective_progress.text: The southern valley. This is where they rest before the final push. Mark this place — it will be empty of predators for three days after the herd passes.

quest_complete.speaker: Scout_Davan
quest_complete.text: You mapped the whole route. River ford, grazing grounds, rest valley. This is worth more than any weapon — this is a safe road south.

quest_complete.speaker: Scout_Davan
quest_complete.text: When the dry season bites hard, we follow the herd. They know where the water is. They have always known.

quest_failed.speaker: Scout_Davan
quest_failed.text: You spooked them. The herd scattered — we lost the route. It will take weeks before they settle into a new pattern. We will have to find another way south.

lore_context.herd_migration_ecology: The Brachiosaurus herd follows a seasonal migration route dictated by water availability and vegetation cycles. This route has been stable for generations — the same paths worn into the earth by thousands of years of movement.
lore_context.why_this_matters: Completing this quest unlocks the 'Migration Map' — a persistent overlay showing safe travel corridors and predator ambush zones along the herd route. This is critical for long-distance exploration.
lore_context.ecological_note: Large herbivore herds function as living ecological sensors. Where they graze calmly, no apex predators are present. Where they bunch and face outward, danger is near. Reading herd behavior is a core survival skill.
